steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The first book costs \$18. Every book after the first costs \$14. If $x$ books are purchased, the number of additional books is $x - 1$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Verify with $x = 1$: $y = 14(0) + 18 = 18$. With $x = 3$: $y = 14(2) + 18 = 46$. Both are correct.

choices.explanation:
• Test $x = 2$: $y = 20$. But the actual cost is $18 + 14 = 32$.
  • This adds $x$ dollars per book rather than \$14 per additional book.
  • Eliminated because the per-book rate is wrong.

• Test $x = 1$: $y = 14 + 18 = 32$. But the first book costs only \$18.
  • This charges \$14 for every book including the first, double-counting the first book's fee.
  • Eliminated because it overcharges for the first book.

• Test $x = 1$: $y = 14(0) + 18 = 18$. Correct. Test $x = 4$: $y = 14(3) + 18 = 60$. Correct.
  • The structure exactly mirrors the problem: \$18 fixed for book 1, \$14 for each of the $(x-1)$ remaining books.
  • That confirms C is correct.

• Test $x = 2$: $y = 1 + 18 = 19$. But the actual cost is 18 + 14 = 32.
  • This charges \$1 per additional book instead of \$14.
  • Eliminated because the \$14 rate for additional books is missing.
